text.title: Discovery Research Handoff Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Discovery Research Handoff Template application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Handoff MUST fit on one page (≤500 words). Longer artefacts go unread by the receiving lead.
rule.rationale: Handoff-research — anchors Discovery Research Handoff Template rule `one-page-cap`.

rule.statement: Handoff MUST contain all five sections: problem statement, persona snapshot, JTBD, evidence, success metric. Missing any section = the handoff is rejected by the receiving lead.
rule.rationale: Discovery-handoff practice — anchors Discovery Research Handoff Template rule `five-required-sections`.

rule.statement: Evidence section MUST contain ≥3 verbatim customer quotes with source attribution (interview-id + date). Paraphrased summaries are rejected.
rule.rationale: Verbatim-evidence research — anchors Discovery Research Handoff Template rule `evidence-three-quotes`.

rule.statement: Handoff MUST be signed by both the PM AND the receiving lead before solutioning starts. Single-signature handoffs lack the shared-understanding gate they were designed to create.
rule.rationale: Handoff-governance practice — anchors Discovery Research Handoff Template rule `dual-signature`.

rule.statement: Success metric MUST be exactly one observable + threshold + window. Multi-metric handoffs hand off scope ambiguity.
rule.rationale: Goal-Question-Metric — anchors Discovery Research Handoff Template rule `success-metric-singular`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
